site_tagline: Historical materials, transcriptions, decipherment studies & holdings
site_description: An open philological knowledge base integrating historical documents, dictionaries, wordlists, oral-literature records and research on the Ainu language.
home_hero_title: Aynu itak kampi opitta, sine uske ta
home_hero_lead: A philological information base for the Ainu language: where the sources are, what they contain, who has read them, and which research surrounds them — across periods, regions and dialects.
people_lead: Authors, recorders, speakers and researchers attested in the sources.
places_lead: Composition places, recording regions and dialect areas of the sources.
institutions_lead: Libraries, museums and research institutes that hold or publish the sources.
timeline_lead: Ainu-language sources by date, from the early Edo period to today.
map_lead: Dialect areas, recording regions and holding institutions of the sources.
map_note: Coordinates are approximate, placed at the centre of each dialect area or institution.
network_lead: A 3D map of how the works cite one another.
about_intro: The Ainu Textual Sources Database collects historical documents, old manuscripts, dictionaries, wordlists, place-name materials, oral-literature records and field notes on the Ainu language, and links their bibliographic data, holdings, digital images, transcriptions, decipherment research, geographic and chronological information. It is not merely a list of materials but an open knowledge base spanning philology, linguistics, history and information science.
about_purpose_p: To make it possible to see at a glance where Ainu-language sources are, what they contain, how they have been read, what research surrounds them, and which region, period, dialect and context they relate to.
about_scope_p: Historical documents and early records of Ainu, vocabularies and dictionaries, place-name materials, oral-literature transcriptions, survey notes, manuscripts and printed editions, as well as secondary literature (transcription, decipherment, philological and linguistic studies) and digital resources (IIIF, library collections, museum databases, transcription projects).
about_model_p: The central entity is the Source (資料). Around it are People, Places, Institutions, external links, tags, source-to-source relations and a full revision history for collaborative editing.
about_data_p: The initial dataset is aggregated from the open ainu-dictionaries, ainu-grammar and ainu-corpora repositories. Bibliographic records can then be corrected and enriched collaboratively, with sourced statements and edit history.
about_contribute_p: Sign in to add new sources or correct existing records. Every edit is versioned, so the history of each record is preserved.
footer_tagline: An open philological knowledge base for Ainu-language sources.
footer_data_credit: Data from ainu-dictionaries, ainu-grammar and ainu-corpora.
